title: SeasoningEntry
description: P-VS-09 (Seasoning queue) entry. A REJECT verdict with no clear revision path is 'laid aside indefinitely' rather than discarded — kept in state/seasoning.jsonl (or lab/sod/seasoning.jsonl post-L-01) for future cycle-recognition revival when context changes. Per Sheeran 1983 ch. 6 + BYM Quaker faith & practice §12.26.

id.description: Stable ID for the seasoning entry. Format: 'season-' + 8 hex chars (truncated SHA of content + ts).

source_dispatch_id.description: ID or path of the dispatch whose REJECT verdict triggered seasoning.

verdict.description: Always REJECT — only REJECT-with-no-revision-path routes to seasoning. APPROVE_WITH_CAVEATS uses concerns_embedded forward-flow instead (P-VS-08).

summary.description: ≥50-char summary of what was rejected and why no clear revision path existed at the time of seasoning.

revival_conditions.description: Concrete observable conditions under which this seasoning entry should be revisited. NOT a calendar timer — bert deliberately uses no auto-revival timer (load-bearing per P-VS-09). Conditions are like 'when free-tier context window exceeds 64K' or 'when an agent benchmark with reward-hacking-resistance methodology emerges'.

cycle.description: Cycle in which this entry was seasoned.

altitude.description: Dispatch altitude of the seasoned work (informs revival prioritization).

tags.description: Free-form tags for cycle-recognition revival queries.

revived_at.description: If/when this entry was revived; null until revival fires.

revival_dispatch_id.description: Dispatch ID of the revived attempt (when revived).
